---
title: REORG TABLE | Databricks on AWS
source: https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/sql/language-manual/delta-reorg-table
ingestedAt: "2026-06-18T08:18:58.951Z"
---

**Applies to:** ![check marked yes](data:image/png;base64,) Databricks SQL ![check marked yes](data:image/png;base64,) Databricks Runtime 11.3 LTS and above

Reorganize a Delta Lake table by rewriting files to purge soft-deleted data, such as the column data dropped by ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN, or by performing Delta Lake checkpointing to improve metadata management.

## Syntax[​](#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

    REORG [ TABLE ] table_name { [ WHERE predicate ] APPLY ( PURGE ) |                             APPLY ( UPGRADE UNIFORM ( ICEBERG_COMPAT_VERSION = version ) |                                     CHECKPOINT ) }

For Databricks Runtime versions before 15.4 `TABLE` is a mandatory keyword.

note

*   `APPLY (PURGE)` only rewrites files that contain soft-deleted data.
*   `APPLY (UPGRADE)` may rewrite all files.
*   `REORG TABLE` is _idempotent_, meaning that if it is run twice on the same dataset, the second run has no effect.
*   After running `APPLY (PURGE)`, the soft-deleted data may still exist in the old files. You can run [VACUUM](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/sql/language-manual/delta-vacuum) to physically delete the old files.
*   `APPLY (CHECKPOINT)` requires the table to have [checkpoint V2](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/tables/features/checkpoint-v2) enabled to prevent corruption caused by race conditions.

## Parameters[​](#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

*   [table\_name](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-names#table-name)
    
    Identifies an existing Delta table. The name must not include a [temporal specification or options specification](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-names#table-name).
    
*   **`WHERE` [predicate](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-expression#boolean-expression)**
    
    For `APPLY (PURGE)`, reorganizes the files that match the given partition predicate. Only filters involving partition key attributes are supported.
    
*   **`APPLY (PURGE)`**
    
    Specifies that the purpose of file rewriting is to purge soft-deleted data. See [Purge metadata-only deletes to force data rewrite](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/tables/operations/vacuum#purge).
    
*   **`APPLY (UPGRADE UNIFORM ( ICEBERG_COMPAT_VERSION = version ))`**
    
    **Applies to:** ![check marked yes](data:image/png;base64,) Databricks SQL ![check marked yes](data:image/png;base64,) Databricks Runtime 14.3 and above
    
    Specifies that the purpose of file rewriting is to upgrade the table to the given Apache Iceberg version. `version` must be either `1` or `2`.
    
*   **`APPLY (CHECKPOINT)`**
    
    **Applies to:** ![check marked yes](data:image/png;base64,) Databricks Runtime 16.3 and above
    
    Performs Delta checkpointing on the table’s latest Delta version.
    

## Examples[​](#examples "Direct link to Examples")

SQL

    > REORG TABLE events APPLY (PURGE);> REORG TABLE events WHERE date >= '2022-01-01' APPLY (PURGE);> REORG TABLE events    WHERE date >= current_timestamp() - INTERVAL '1' DAY    APPLY (PURGE);> REORG TABLE events APPLY (UPGRADE UNIFORM(ICEBERG_COMPAT_VERSION=2));> REORG TABLE events APPLY (CHECKPOINT);

## Related[​](#related "Direct link to Related")

*   [Remove unused data files with vacuum](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/tables/operations/vacuum)
